ogTitle: Wurdong Heights vs Wuruma Dam: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wurdong Heights and Wuruma Dam.
heading: Wurdong Heights vs Wuruma Dam — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wurdong Heights, Queensland and Wuruma Dam, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wuruma Dam has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Wurdong Heights (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wurdong Heights or Wuruma Dam safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wurdong Heights has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Wuruma Dam scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wurdong Heights scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Wuruma Dam scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wurdong Heights or Wuruma Dam?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wuruma Dam has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
